two_min_version_en: Drink one full glass of water right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在就喝一杯水
description_what_to_do_en: Drink 8 glasses (approx 2 litres) of water throughout the day. Log each glass as you go. Includes plain water, herbal tea, and sparkling water — not coffee or alcohol.
description_what_to_do_cn: 全天喝 8 杯水（约 2 升）。每喝一杯就记一次。包括白水、草本茶、气泡水 — 不包括咖啡和酒精。
why_it_works_en: Supports kidney function, improves skin health, aids digestion, reduces fatigue, improves concentration, prevents headaches, and supports metabolism.
why_it_works_cn: 支持肾功能，改善皮肤健康，帮助消化，减少疲劳，提高专注力，预防头痛，支持新陈代谢。
notes_for_product: Consider cups/ml toggle. Default 8 glasses. Auto-track if smart bottle integration added later.

two_min_version_en: Put your supplements next to something you do every morning
two_min_version_cn: 把补充剂放在你每天早上都会做的事情旁边
description_what_to_do_en: Take your daily supplements or medication at the same time each day — vitamins, omega-3, creatine, protein shake, or any health supplement stack. The habit is the routine, not the specific supplement.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天在同一时间服用补充剂或药物 — 维生素、鱼油、肌酸、蛋白粉或任何保健品。习惯的是规律，而不是具体补充什么。
why_it_works_en: Addresses nutritional gaps in modern diets. Vitamin D deficiency affects ~40% of adults. Consistency is the key driver of benefit — sporadic supplementation is largely ineffective.
why_it_works_cn: 弥补现代饮食的营养缺口。约 40% 的成年人缺乏维生素 D。规律性是收益的关键 — 偶尔补充效果甚微。
notes_for_product: Simple Y/N. Consider allowing users to name their stack for personalisation.

two_min_version_en: Open your front door and stand outside for 30 seconds
two_min_version_cn: 打开家门，在外面站 30 秒
description_what_to_do_en: Step outside for fresh air and a brief change of environment — even 2 minutes counts. No exercise required. Just get outdoors.
description_what_to_do_cn: 出门呼吸新鲜空气，换个环境 — 哪怕 2 分钟也算。不需要运动，只要到户外就好。
why_it_works_en: Fresh air and daylight exposure improve mood and circadian rhythm. Breaking indoor confinement reduces eye strain, refreshes mental state, and provides a natural cognitive reset.
why_it_works_cn: 新鲜空气和日光暴露改善情绪和昼夜节律。打破室内束缚减轻眼睛疲劳，刷新精神状态，提供自然的认知重置。
notes_for_product: Minimum viable outdoor habit. Pairs well with Morning sunlight.

two_min_version_en: Floss just one gap between two teeth — that's a start
two_min_version_cn: 只清洁两颗牙之间的缝隙 — 这就是开始
description_what_to_do_en: Floss between your teeth at least once today — ideally before bedtime brushing.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天至少用一次牙线 — 最好在睡前刷牙前。
why_it_works_en: Removes plaque from areas a toothbrush cannot reach; reduces risk of gum disease, tooth decay, and bad breath. Long-term oral hygiene correlates with reduced risk of cardiovascular disease.
why_it_works_cn: 清除牙刷刷不到区域的牙菌斑；降低牙龈疾病、蛀牙和口臭风险。长期口腔卫生与降低心血管疾病风险相关。
notes_for_product: Low-friction oral health habit. Gateway habit to better self-care.

two_min_version_en: Walk around the block — that's about 1,000 steps
two_min_version_cn: 绕着街区走一圈 — 大约 1,000 步
description_what_to_do_en: Walk enough throughout the day to reach 5,000 steps. This can be achieved through deliberate walks, commuting on foot, taking stairs, or any combination of daily movement. Auto-track from phone or wearable where possible.
description_what_to_do_cn: 全天步行达到 5,000 步。可以通过刻意散步、步行通勤、走楼梯或任何日常活动组合实现。尽可能从手机或穿戴设备自动同步。
why_it_works_en: Reduces risk of cardiovascular disease, type 2 diabetes, and all-cause mortality. Improves mood, supports weight management. Studies show 7,000–8,000 steps/day reduces mortality risk by 50–70% vs sedentary baseline.
why_it_works_cn: 降低心血管疾病、2 型糖尿病和全因死亡率风险。改善情绪，支持体重管理。研究表明每天 7,000–8,000 步比久坐基线降低 50–70% 死亡风险。
notes_for_product: Auto-sync from Apple Health / Google Fit. Show progress ring.

two_min_version_en: Stand up and do 5 slow neck rolls in each direction
two_min_version_cn: 站起来，向每个方向慢慢转动脖子 5 次
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 5 minutes on deliberate stretching or mobility work — static stretches, dynamic warm-up, foam rolling, or yoga flows. Focus on tight areas: neck, shoulders, hips, hamstrings.
description_what_to_do_cn: 花 5 分钟做针对性的拉伸或活动度训练 — 静态拉伸、动态热身、泡沫轴或瑜伽流。重点关注紧张区域：颈部、肩部、髋部、腘绳肌。
why_it_works_en: Improves range of motion, reduces injury risk, relieves muscle tension from desk work, reduces chronic pain, supports athletic performance, and improves posture. Regular stretching reduces musculoskeletal injury risk by 54%.
why_it_works_cn: 改善活动范围，降低受伤风险，缓解久坐带来的肌肉紧张，减少慢性疼痛，支持运动表现，改善体态。定期拉伸降低 54% 肌肉骨骼损伤风险。
notes_for_product: Low time commitment. Can be done at desk.

two_min_version_en: Do 10 bodyweight squats right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在做 10 个自重深蹲
description_what_to_do_en: Complete a workout session today — this can be strength training, cardio, HIIT, yoga, swimming, cycling, or any intentional exercise beyond walking. Log the session as complete.
description_what_to_do_cn: 完成一次锻炼 — 可以是力量训练、有氧、HIIT、瑜伽、游泳、骑行或任何步行之外的针对性运动。记录为完成。
why_it_works_en: Improves cardiovascular health, builds muscle and bone density, boosts mood through endorphins, increases energy levels, improves sleep quality, reduces risk of chronic disease, and supports weight management.
why_it_works_cn: 改善心血管健康，增强肌肉和骨密度，通过内啡肽提升情绪，增加能量水平，改善睡眠质量，降低慢性病风险，支持体重管理。
notes_for_product: Optional text for workout type. Default Y/N completion.

two_min_version_en: Take the stairs for just one floor today
two_min_version_cn: 今天只走一层楼梯
description_what_to_do_en: Choose the stairs instead of the elevator or escalator at least once today.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天至少选择一次走楼梯而不是电梯或扶梯。
why_it_works_en: Adds incidental physical activity to daily life without requiring dedicated exercise time. Improves cardiovascular health, strengthens leg muscles, and burns extra calories. Studies show taking stairs burns 3–5x more calories than using elevator.
why_it_works_cn: 为日常生活增加偶然的身体活动，不需要专门的运动时间。改善心血管健康，增强腿部肌肉，燃烧额外卡路里。研究表明走楼梯比乘电梯多消耗 3–5 倍卡路里。
notes_for_product: Classic 'small step' habit.

two_min_version_en: Ask yourself: did I eat one real meal today?
two_min_version_cn: 问自己：今天我吃了一顿正经饭吗？
description_what_to_do_en: At the end of the day, reflect: did you eat at least one meal today that felt nourishing and satisfying? A simple yes/no check-in.
description_what_to_do_cn: 一天结束时反思：今天是否至少吃了一餐感觉滋养且满足的饭？简单的 yes/no 打卡。
why_it_works_en: Builds nutritional self-awareness without the burden of logging. Daily reflection is a proven first step toward behavior change. Self-monitoring is consistently identified as one of the most effective behavior-change strategies.
why_it_works_cn: 在没有记录负担的情况下建立营养自我意识。每日反思是行为改变的 proven 第一步。自我监控一直被认为是最有效的行为改变策略之一。
notes_for_product: Y/N reflection. Lower barrier than calorie tracking.

two_min_version_en: Eat one piece of fruit or a handful of raw vegetables right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在吃一块水果或一把生蔬菜
description_what_to_do_en: Eat at least one serving of vegetables (or fruit) as part of a meal today. This can be a side dish, part of a main course, or a snack.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天至少吃一份蔬菜（或水果）作为一餐的一部分。可以是配菜、主菜的一部分或零食。
why_it_works_en: Provides essential vitamins, minerals, and fibre; supports digestion, immunity, and long-term health. Most adults do not meet recommended daily vegetable intake. WHO recommends at least 400g of fruits and vegetables per day.
why_it_works_cn: 提供必需的维生素、矿物质和纤维；支持消化、免疫力和长期健康。大多数成年人没有达到每日推荐的蔬菜摄入量。世界卫生组织建议每天至少摄入 400 克水果和蔬菜。
notes_for_product: Low-friction entry point to better nutrition.

two_min_version_en: Make tomorrow's lunch tonight — it takes 5 minutes
two_min_version_cn: 今晚做明天的午餐 — 只需 5 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Cook or prepare at least one meal at home today — breakfast, lunch, or dinner counts. Includes simple assembly (overnight oats, salad) not just cooking from scratch.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天在家烹饪或准备至少一顿饭 — 早餐、午餐或晚餐都算。包括简单组装（隔夜燕麦、沙拉），不限于从头烹饪。
why_it_works_en: Reduces food spend by average $300–500/month vs eating out, improves nutritional quality, reduces calorie intake (restaurant meals average 134% of daily calorie recommendation), builds cooking confidence over time.
why_it_works_cn: 相比外出就餐，平均每月减少 300–500 美元食品支出，改善营养质量，减少卡路里摄入（餐厅餐食平均为每日推荐卡路里的 134%），随时间建立烹饪信心。
notes_for_product: Optional text for what you cooked.

two_min_version_en: Eat one bite of anything within the next hour
two_min_version_cn: 接下来一小时内吃一口任何东西
description_what_to_do_en: Eat something within 2 hours of waking up — anything counts. The habit is breaking the fast, not perfection.
description_what_to_do_cn: 起床后 2 小时内吃点东西 — 任何东西都算。习惯是打破禁食，不是追求完美。
why_it_works_en: Breakfast consumption is associated with improved concentration, better blood sugar control, and reduced likelihood of overeating later in the day. Regular breakfast eaters have better overall nutrient intake.
why_it_works_cn: 吃早餐与改善专注力、更好的血糖控制以及降低当天晚些时候暴饮暴食的可能性相关。规律吃早餐的人整体营养摄入更好。
notes_for_product: Very low barrier. Can be disabled by intermittent fasters.

two_min_version_en: Just log what you eat for breakfast today — nothing else
two_min_version_cn: 只记录今天早餐吃了什么 — 其他都不用
description_what_to_do_en: Log your food intake for the day — total calories, or macros (protein, carbs, fat). Use any method: Stalio's log, MyFitnessPal integration, or a manual entry.
description_what_to_do_cn: 记录当天的食物摄入 — 总卡路里或宏量营养素（蛋白质、碳水化合物、脂肪）。使用任何方法：Stalio 记录、MyFitnessPal 集成或手动输入。
why_it_works_en: Creates precise nutritional awareness. Required for body recomposition goals (muscle gain, fat loss). Helps identify hidden calories and eating patterns invisible to casual reflection.
why_it_works_cn: 创造精确的营养意识。身体重塑目标（增肌、减脂）所需。帮助识别随意反思无法发现的隐藏卡路里和饮食模式。
notes_for_product: High-friction habit. Link to MyFitnessPal API.

two_min_version_en: Go to bed 15 minutes earlier than last night
two_min_version_cn: 比昨晚早睡 15 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Go to bed before 11 PM. Log your bedtime. The habit is the timing, not just the duration.
description_what_to_do_cn: 晚上 11 点前上床睡觉。记录上床时间。习惯是时间点，不仅仅是时长。
why_it_works_en: Early bedtime is associated with better sleep quality, deeper REM sleep, improved mood, better cognitive function, and reduced risk of metabolic disorders. Consistent early bedtimes strengthen circadian rhythm.
why_it_works_cn: 早睡与更好的睡眠质量、更深的 REM 睡眠、改善情绪、更好的认知功能以及降低代谢紊乱风险相关。规律的早睡时间增强昼夜节律。
notes_for_product: Log bedtime. Surface consistency score.

two_min_version_en: Set your alarm for the same time tomorrow — even weekend
two_min_version_cn: 把明天的闹钟设在同一个时间 — 即使是周末
description_what_to_do_en: Wake up within the same 30-minute window each day (including weekends). Log your wake time.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天在同一 30 分钟时间窗口内起床（包括周末）。记录起床时间。
why_it_works_en: Consistent wake time is the single strongest lever for circadian health. It sets your body's internal clock, improves sleep quality, enhances daytime alertness, and regulates appetite and metabolism.
why_it_works_cn: 规律的起床时间是昼夜节律健康的最强杠杆。它设定你身体的内在时钟，改善睡眠质量，增强白天警觉性，调节食欲和新陈代谢。
notes_for_product: Log wake time. Weekend consistency is key.

two_min_version_en: Dim every light in your home right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在把家里所有的灯调暗
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 30 minutes before bed in a calm, screen-free activity — reading (physical book), stretching, journaling, herbal tea, or conversation. No phones, no laptops, no TV.
description_what_to_do_cn: 睡前 30 分钟进行平静的、无屏幕的活动 — 阅读（纸质书）、拉伸、写日记、喝草本茶或交谈。不看手机、不看电脑、不看电视。
why_it_works_en: Signals your nervous system to transition from alert to rest mode, reduces sleep latency (time to fall asleep), improves sleep quality and depth, reduces next-day anxiety. Blue light before bed suppresses melatonin for up to 3 hours.
why_it_works_cn: 向你的神经系统发出从警觉过渡到休息模式的信号，减少入睡潜伏期（入睡所需时间），改善睡眠质量和深度，减少第二天的焦虑。睡前蓝光会抑制褪黑素长达 3 小时。
notes_for_product: Y/N. Pairs with Sleep before 11 PM.

two_min_version_en: Rate last night's sleep from 1 to 5 right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在给昨晚的睡眠评分 1 到 5
description_what_to_do_en: Each morning, rate your sleep quality on a 1–5 scale (1 = terrible, 5 = excellent). Takes 10 seconds.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天早上，用 1–5 分评价你的睡眠质量（1 = 很差，5 = 极佳）。只需 10 秒。
why_it_works_en: Sleep quality correlates with physical and mental health more strongly than sleep duration. Tracking builds awareness of what improves or worsens your sleep, enabling better sleep hygiene decisions over time.
why_it_works_cn: 睡眠质量与身心健康的关联比睡眠时长更强。追踪建立对什么改善或恶化睡眠的认知，随时间做出更好的睡眠卫生决策。
notes_for_product: Simple scale. Surface correlations with other habits.

two_min_version_en: Read the next page of whatever book is nearest you
two_min_version_cn: 读离你最近的书下一页
description_what_to_do_en: Read for 15 minutes from any book — fiction, non-fiction, biography. Audiobooks count. The habit is daily engagement with long-form reading.
description_what_to_do_cn: 读任何书 15 分钟 — 小说、非虚构、传记。有声书也算。习惯是每天参与长文阅读。
why_it_works_en: Expands vocabulary, builds knowledge, improves empathy (fiction), reduces stress (reading for 6 minutes reduces stress by 68%), improves sleep when replacing screen time before bed.
why_it_works_cn: 扩展词汇量，建立知识，提高共情能力（小说），减轻压力（阅读 6 分钟减轻 68% 压力），替代睡前屏幕时间时改善睡眠。
notes_for_product: Allow pages or minutes. Evening anchor most popular.

two_min_version_en: Sit still for 2 minutes and count your breaths
two_min_version_cn: 静坐 2 分钟，数呼吸
description_what_to_do_en: Sit quietly for 5–10 minutes with no phone, no music, no input. Focus on your breath or simply be still. Can be guided or unguided.
description_what_to_do_cn: 静坐 5–10 分钟，不看手机、不听音乐、没有输入。专注于呼吸或只是静止。可以有引导或无引导。
why_it_works_en: Reduces cortisol (stress hormone), improves emotional regulation, increases focus and attention span, reduces symptoms of anxiety and depression, improves self-awareness. Harvard neuroscience: 8 weeks of daily meditation measurably changes gray matter density in prefrontal cortex.
why_it_works_cn: 减少皮质醇（压力激素），改善情绪调节，提高专注力和注意力广度，减少焦虑和抑郁症状，提高自我意识。哈佛神经科学：每天冥想 8 周可显著改变前额叶灰质密度。
notes_for_product: Deliberately low time commitment.

two_min_version_en: Name one thing that went well today
two_min_version_cn: 说出今天顺利的一件事
description_what_to_do_en: Write down or mentally note 3 things you are genuinely grateful for today. Be specific — 'the way my coffee tasted this morning' is more effective than 'my family'.
description_what_to_do_cn: 写下或心里记下今天你真正感恩的 3 件事。要具体 — '今天早上咖啡的味道'比'我的家人'更有效。
why_it_works_en: Increases subjective wellbeing, reduces depressive symptoms, improves relationship satisfaction, shifts attentional bias toward positive aspects of life. Daily gratitude associated with reduced inflammation markers.
why_it_works_cn: 提高主观幸福感，减少抑郁症状，改善关系满意度，将注意力偏向生活的积极方面。日常感恩与减少炎症标志物相关。
notes_for_product: MUST write 3 specific items. Multi-field input.

two_min_version_en: Ask someone: 'What's one thing you learned recently?'
two_min_version_cn: 问别人：'你最近学到的一件事是什么？'
description_what_to_do_en: Learn one new thing today — a fact, a word, a concept, a skill. Write down what you learned in one sentence.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天学一样新东西 — 一个事实、一个词、一个概念、一个技能。用一句话写下你学到了什么。
why_it_works_en: Continuous learning is associated with cognitive resilience, delayed cognitive decline, and greater life satisfaction. The act of writing down what you learned reinforces retention through the testing effect.
why_it_works_cn: 持续学习与认知韧性、延迟认知衰退和更高生活满意度相关。写下所学内容的行为通过测试效应强化记忆。
notes_for_product: MUST write one sentence of new learning.

two_min_version_en: Doodle on a piece of paper for 2 minutes
two_min_version_cn: 在一张纸上涂鸦 2 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 15 minutes on a calming, repetitive creative activity — coloring a mandala, drawing patterns, painting, or any low-stakes artistic expression. The goal is process, not product.
description_what_to_do_cn: 花 15 分钟进行平静的、重复性的创意活动 — 给曼陀罗涂色、画图案、画画或任何低风险的艺术表达。目标是过程，不是作品。
why_it_works_en: Repetitive creative activities induce a meditative state similar to mindfulness, reduce cortisol, lower anxiety, and provide a sense of accomplishment without performance pressure. Art therapy principles show measurable stress reduction.
why_it_works_cn: 重复性创意活动诱导类似正念的冥想状态，减少皮质醇，降低焦虑，并在没有表现压力的情况下提供成就感。艺术治疗原则显示可衡量的压力减少。
notes_for_product: Low pressure creative outlet.

two_min_version_en: Write one sentence about how you feel right now
two_min_version_cn: 写一句话描述你现在的感受
description_what_to_do_en: Write one note today about anything — what happened, what you're thinking, what you noticed. No length requirement. One sentence counts.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天写一则关于任何事情的笔记 — 发生了什么、你在想什么、你注意到了什么。没有长度要求。一句话也算。
why_it_works_en: Expressive writing reduces stress and anxiety, improves emotional processing, builds self-awareness, and clarifies thinking. Even very brief journaling has been shown to have psychological benefits.
why_it_works_cn: 表达性写作减少压力和焦虑，改善情绪处理，建立自我意识，澄清思维。即使是极短的日记也被证明有心理益处。
notes_for_product: MUST write something. Gateway to journaling.

two_min_version_en: Rate your day from 1 to 5 right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在给你的一天评分 1 到 5
description_what_to_do_en: Once per day — typically in the evening — rate your overall mood on a 1–5 scale and optionally add a one-line note about why. Takes under 60 seconds.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天一次 — 通常在晚上 — 用 1–5 分评价你的整体心情，并可选添加一行说明为什么。只需不到 60 秒。
why_it_works_en: Builds emotional self-awareness, helps identify mood patterns linked to sleep, exercise, diet, or social connection, provides data for therapy, and creates a longitudinal record of mental health trends.
why_it_works_cn: 建立情绪自我意识，帮助识别与睡眠、运动、饮食或社交联系相关的心情模式，为治疗提供数据，并创建心理健康趋势的纵向记录。
notes_for_product: Scale required. Text optional.

two_min_version_en: Think of one thing that went well today — that's it
two_min_version_cn: 想一件今天顺利的事 — 就这样
description_what_to_do_en: Write down one thing you are grateful for today. Be specific. That's it.
description_what_to_do_cn: 写下今天你感激的一件事。要具体。就这样。
why_it_works_en: Gratitude practice increases subjective wellbeing, reduces depressive symptoms, and shifts attention toward positive aspects of life. Even a single daily gratitude has measurable benefits.
why_it_works_cn: 感恩练习提高主观幸福感，减少抑郁症状，将注意力转向生活的积极方面。即使每天只感恩一件事也有可衡量的益处。
notes_for_product: MUST write one gratitude. Lower barrier than H020.

two_min_version_en: Write down one thing you learned about yourself today
two_min_version_cn: 写下你今天了解到的关于自己的一件事
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 5 minutes at the end of the day reflecting: what went well today? What could have gone better? What's one thing to do differently tomorrow?
description_what_to_do_cn: 一天结束时花 5 分钟反思：今天什么顺利？什么可以更好？明天有什么可以做得不同？
why_it_works_en: Closes open mental loops (reduces rumination and improves sleep), builds self-accountability, surfaces patterns over time, and prepares the mind for effective next-day start.
why_it_works_cn: 关闭开放的心理循环（减少反刍思维，改善睡眠），建立自我责任感，随时间浮现模式，为有效的第二天开始做好准备。
notes_for_product: MUST write reflection. Structured prompting.

two_min_version_en: Call one family member for just 2 minutes
two_min_version_cn: 给一位家人打电话，只说 2 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Call a family member today — parent, sibling, grandparent, or any relative. A voice call, not just a text.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天给一位家人打电话 — 父母、兄弟姐妹、祖父母或任何亲戚。是语音通话，不仅仅是短信。
why_it_works_en: Maintains family bonds across distance, provides emotional support both ways, reduces loneliness, and builds a reliable family connection habit. Regular family calls correlate with higher reported life satisfaction.
why_it_works_cn: 跨越距离维持家庭纽带，提供双向情感支持，减少孤独感，建立可靠的家庭联系习惯。规律的家庭通话与更高的生活满意度相关。
notes_for_product: Optional text for who you called.

two_min_version_en: Text one person right now who you haven't spoken to this week
two_min_version_cn: 现在就给本周没说过话的人发条消息
description_what_to_do_en: Send a personal message, make a call, or have a meaningful conversation with one friend today — not a reply to a message they sent, but an initiation.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天给一位朋友发送个人消息、打电话或进行有意义的对话 — 不是回复他们发的消息，而是主动发起。
why_it_works_en: Combats loneliness, strengthens relationships proactively rather than reactively, builds social capital, and improves sense of belonging and subjective wellbeing. Proactive outreach more relationship-building than reactive replies.
why_it_works_cn: 对抗孤独感，主动而非被动地加强关系，建立社会资本，提高归属感和主观幸福感。主动联系比被动回复更能建立关系。
notes_for_product: Optional text for who.

two_min_version_en: Put phones away and talk to your partner for 5 minutes
two_min_version_cn: 把手机收起来，和伴侣交谈 5 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Spend intentional, distraction-free time with your partner — 15 minutes of conversation, a walk together, cooking together, or any shared activity with no phones.
description_what_to_do_cn: 与伴侣一起度过专注的、无干扰的时间 — 15 分钟对话、一起散步、一起做饭或任何无手机的共同活动。
why_it_works_en: Quality time is the strongest predictor of relationship satisfaction. Undivided attention builds emotional intimacy, reduces relationship stress, and creates shared positive experiences that buffer against conflict.
why_it_works_cn: 高质量时光是关系满意度的最强预测因素。专注的关注建立情感亲密感，减少关系压力，并创造缓冲冲突的共享积极体验。
notes_for_product: Optional text for activity.

two_min_version_en: Put your phone in another room and ask your child about their day
two_min_version_cn: 把手机放在另一个房间，问你的孩子今天过得怎么样
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 15 minutes of undivided attention with your child — no phone, no TV, no multitasking. Just being present with them, following their lead.
description_what_to_do_cn: 花 15 分钟全心关注你的孩子 — 不看手机、不看电视、不一心多用。只是陪伴他们，跟随他们的引导。
why_it_works_en: Undivided parental attention builds secure attachment, improves child behavior, strengthens parent-child bond, and models healthy presence. Research shows children need as little as 10–15 minutes of quality focused attention per day to feel valued.
why_it_works_cn: 全心关注建立安全型依恋，改善孩子行为，加强亲子纽带，示范健康的存在。研究表明孩子每天只需要 10–15 分钟的高质量专注关注就能感到被重视。
notes_for_product: For users with children.

habit_name_cn: 无电子设备的家庭时光
two_min_version_en: Put every phone in the house in another room for 30 minutes
two_min_version_cn: 把家里所有手机放在另一个房间 30 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Spend a defined block of time with family where all devices are put away — in another room, not just face-down. Meals, games, walks, or conversation.
description_what_to_do_cn: 与家人共度一段所有人都不使用电子设备的时间 — 放在另一个房间，不只是扣在桌上。吃饭、游戏、散步或交谈。
why_it_works_en: Face-down phones on dinner tables reduce conversation quality and connection even when not actively used. Family meals without screens associated with better child outcomes across academic, social, and emotional dimensions.
why_it_works_cn: 餐桌上扣着的手机即使不被主动使用也会降低对话质量和连接。没有屏幕的家庭餐与孩子在学业、社交和情感方面的更好表现相关。
notes_for_product: Higher commitment. Ideal for weekly dinners.

two_min_version_en: Send one genuine compliment to someone right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在就给某人发一句真诚的赞美
description_what_to_do_en: Do one deliberate act of kindness today — for a stranger, friend, family member, or colleague. It can be small: a compliment, helping with a task, buying someone a coffee, writing a thank-you note.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天做一件有意的善事 — 为陌生人、朋友、家人或同事。可以很小：一句赞美、帮忙做一件事、给别人买杯咖啡、写一张感谢卡。
why_it_works_en: Increases personal happiness (giving activates the same reward pathways as receiving), reduces stress, strengthens social bonds, and creates a positive feedback loop in communities and relationships.
why_it_works_cn: 增加个人幸福感（给予激活与接受相同的奖励通路），减轻压力，加强社会纽带，在社区和关系中创造正向反馈循环。
notes_for_product: Optional text for what you did.

two_min_version_en: Charge your phone on the other side of the room tonight
two_min_version_cn: 今晚把手机放在房间另一头充电
description_what_to_do_en: Do not use or look at your phone while in bed. The bed is for sleep and intimacy only. Leave your phone on a desk or in another room.
description_what_to_do_cn: 躺在床上时不使用或看手机。床只用于睡眠和亲密。把手机放在书桌或另一个房间。
why_it_works_en: Phone use in bed disrupts sleep through blue light (melatonin suppression), cognitive activation (harder to fall asleep), and poor sleep hygiene conditioning (associating bed with phone scrolling, not sleep).
why_it_works_cn: 躺在床上看手机通过蓝光（抑制褪黑素）、认知激活（更难入睡）和不良睡眠卫生条件反射（将床与刷手机而非睡眠关联）干扰睡眠。
notes_for_product: One of the highest-leverage digital wellness habits.

two_min_version_en: Delete social apps from your home screen — put them in a folder
two_min_version_cn: 把社交应用从主屏幕删除 — 放进一个文件夹
description_what_to_do_en: Do not open any social media app (Instagram, TikTok, X/Twitter, LinkedIn, Reddit, YouTube) before 12 PM (noon).
description_what_to_do_cn: 中午 12 点前不打开任何社交媒体应用（Instagram、TikTok、X/Twitter、LinkedIn、Reddit、YouTube）。
why_it_works_en: Protects morning cognitive energy from comparison, outrage, and dopamine spikes that reduce motivation for deep work. Social media in the morning sets a reactive (not proactive) mental frame for the entire day.
why_it_works_cn: 保护早晨的认知能量免受比较、愤怒和减少深度工作动力的大脑奖赏系统刺激。早晨刷社交媒体为整个一天设置被动（而非主动）的心理框架。
notes_for_product: Allow custom 'social-free until' time.

two_min_version_en: Pour a non-alcoholic drink tonight instead
two_min_version_cn: 今晚倒一杯无酒精饮料代替
description_what_to_do_en: Track each day you do not consume alcohol. This is an avoidance habit — the goal is to accumulate sober days, reduce frequency of drinking, or complete a defined sober challenge.
description_what_to_do_cn: 记录你每天不饮酒的日子。这是一个回避习惯 — 目标是积累清醒天数，减少饮酒频率，或完成特定的清醒挑战（如 Dry January）。
why_it_works_en: Improves sleep quality (alcohol suppresses REM sleep), supports liver health, improves mood stability, reduces cancer risk, supports weight management, and improves athletic performance. Even reducing frequency has significant health benefits.
why_it_works_cn: 改善睡眠质量（酒精抑制 REM 睡眠），支持肝脏健康，改善情绪稳定性，降低癌症风险，支持体重管理，提高运动表现。即使只是减少频率也有显著健康益处。
notes_for_product: Track sober days. Show streak.

two_min_version_en: Skip one sugary thing you usually have today
two_min_version_cn: 今天跳过你通常吃的某样含糖食物
description_what_to_do_en: Avoid added sugar today — no candy, soda, desserts, pastries, or sweetened drinks. Natural sugars from fruit are fine.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天避免添加糖 — 不吃糖果、汽水、甜点、糕点或含糖饮料。水果中的天然糖分没问题。
why_it_works_en: Excess added sugar consumption is linked to obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, and inflammation. Even short sugar reduction breaks the craving cycle and resets taste preferences.
why_it_works_cn: 过量添加糖摄入与肥胖、2 型糖尿病、心血管疾病和炎症相关。即使是短期的减糖也能打破渴望循环并重置口味偏好。
notes_for_product: Avoidance habit.

two_min_version_en: Don't have one cigarette today — just today
two_min_version_cn: 今天不抽一支烟 — 就今天
description_what_to_do_en: Track each day you do not smoke. This is an avoidance habit — the goal is to accumulate smoke-free days toward quitting.
description_what_to_do_cn: 记录你每天不吸烟的日子。这是一个回避习惯 — 目标是积累无烟天数，朝着戒烟前进。
why_it_works_en: Smoking cessation reduces lung cancer risk by 50% within 10 years, cardiovascular risk drops within 1 year, breathing improves within weeks. Average smoker saves $3,000–5,000/year after quitting.
why_it_works_cn: 戒烟在 10 年内降低 50% 肺癌风险，心血管风险在 1 年内下降，呼吸在几周内改善。平均吸烟者戒烟后每年节省 3,000–5,000 美元。
notes_for_product: Day counter. Milestone celebrations.

two_min_version_en: Decide right now: will today be a no-spend day?
two_min_version_cn: 现在决定：今天要不要不花钱？
description_what_to_do_en: Complete a full day without spending money on non-essential items. Necessary expenses (groceries, transport, bills) are excluded. The goal is to consciously break the daily spending habit.
description_what_to_do_cn: 完成一整天不在非必需品上花钱。必要支出（食品杂货、交通、账单）不包括。目标是有意识地打破日常消费习惯。
why_it_works_en: Builds financial willpower, reveals how much discretionary spending is habitual rather than intentional, accumulates meaningful savings over a month, and creates a new relationship with the impulse to buy.
why_it_works_cn: 建立财务意志力，揭示有多少可自由支配支出是习惯性的而非有意的，在一个月内积累有意义的储蓄，并与购买冲动建立新的关系。
notes_for_product: Streak of no-spend days is motivating.

two_min_version_en: Set a 30-minute timer before opening any social app tonight
two_min_version_cn: 今晚打开任何社交应用前设置一个 30 分钟计时器
description_what_to_do_en: Keep total recreational screen time (social media, video streaming, browsing) under a defined daily limit. Excludes work-related screen use.
description_what_to_do_cn: 将总娱乐屏幕时间（社交媒体、视频流、浏览）控制在定义的每日限制内。不包括工作相关的屏幕使用。
why_it_works_en: Reduces anxiety, improves sleep quality, reclaims time for meaningful activities, reduces social comparison and its negative psychological effects, improves real-world attention span.
why_it_works_cn: 减少焦虑，改善睡眠质量，为有意义的活动找回时间，减少社交比较及其负面心理影响，改善现实世界的注意力持续时间。
notes_for_product: Pull from Screen Time API. Allow custom limit.

two_min_version_en: Open Duolingo and complete one lesson right now
two_min_version_cn: 打开 Duolingo 完成一课
description_what_to_do_en: Study or practise a foreign language for a defined duration — Duolingo, Babbel, Anki flashcards, conversation with a tutor, or self-study with a textbook or podcast.
description_what_to_do_cn: 学习或练习外语一段固定时间 — Duolingo、Babbel、Anki 闪卡、与导师对话或使用教科书/播客自学。
why_it_works_en: Builds a valuable cognitive and professional skill, delays cognitive decline (bilingualism delays Alzheimer's onset by ~5 years), opens travel and career opportunities, provides a daily sense of measurable progress.
why_it_works_cn: 建立有价值的认知和职业技能，延迟认知衰退（双语能力延迟阿尔茨海默病发作约 5 年），打开旅行和职业机会，提供日常可衡量的进步感。
notes_for_product: Optional text for what you practiced.

two_min_version_en: Open your course and do 5 minutes — just 5
two_min_version_cn: 打开你的课程做 5 分钟 — 就 5 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 30 minutes of focused time on a specific skill you are developing — coding, design, finance, music, writing, or any other structured learning.
description_what_to_do_cn: 花 30 分钟专注时间在你正在发展的特定技能上 — 编程、设计、金融、音乐、写作或任何其他结构化学习。
why_it_works_en: Career advancement, intellectual satisfaction, keeps the mind engaged and growing, builds identity as a lifelong learner, directly tied to earning potential and professional relevance.
why_it_works_cn: 职业发展，智力满足，保持思维投入和成长，建立终身学习者的身份，与收入潜力和职业相关性直接相关。
notes_for_product: Allow naming the skill. Cumulative hours is key.

two_min_version_en: Write down the one most important thing for today
two_min_version_cn: 写下今天最重要的一件事
description_what_to_do_en: Each morning, write down or review the 3 most important things you need to accomplish today. Do this before opening email or messages. Keep the list to exactly 3.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天早晨，写下或回顾你今天需要完成的 3 件最重要的事。在打开邮件或消息之前做。清单精确保持 3 件。
why_it_works_en: Reduces decision fatigue throughout the day, ensures high-leverage work gets done before reactive work takes over, creates a clear daily success definition, and builds a planning identity.
why_it_works_cn: 减少全天决策疲劳，确保高杠杆工作在被动的日常事务接管之前完成，创建清晰的每日成功定义，建立规划身份。
notes_for_product: MUST write 3 priorities. Multi-field input.

two_min_version_en: Make one small thing right now — even a rough draft
two_min_version_cn: 现在做一件小事 — 即使是草稿
description_what_to_do_en: Spend 30 minutes on your primary creative pursuit — writing, music, art, design, photography, filmmaking, or any craft. It counts only if you made something, not just consumed.
description_what_to_do_cn: 花 30 分钟在你的主要创意追求上 — 写作、音乐、艺术、设计、摄影、电影制作或任何手艺。只有当你创作了东西才算，不仅仅是消费。
why_it_works_en: Builds creative confidence and skill through repetition, reduces creative anxiety (the myth of waiting for inspiration), creates a body of work over time, and provides deep intrinsic satisfaction and flow states.
why_it_works_cn: 通过重复建立创意信心和技能，减少创意焦虑（等待灵感的迷思），随时间创造作品集，提供深刻的内在满足感和心流状态。
notes_for_product: Optional text for what you created.

two_min_version_en: Write one sentence: what's one thing I noticed or learned today?
two_min_version_cn: 写一句话：我今天注意到或学到的一件事是什么？
description_what_to_do_en: At the end of each day, write one sentence about something new you learned — from reading, work, a conversation, a podcast, or observation. A micro-reflection habit.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每天结束时，用一句话写下你学到的新东西 — 来自阅读、工作、对话、播客或观察。一个微反思习惯。
why_it_works_en: Reinforces retention of new information through active recall, builds a curiosity identity, creates a valuable personal knowledge log over time, and makes learning feel tangible and cumulative.
why_it_works_cn: 通过主动回忆强化新信息的记忆，建立好奇心身份，随时间创造有价值的个人知识日志，让学习感觉切实和可累积。
notes_for_product: MUST write one sentence. Lightweight.

two_min_version_en: Close all browser tabs except one and work for 25 minutes
two_min_version_cn: 关闭除一个之外的所有浏览器标签，工作 25 分钟
description_what_to_do_en: Complete one block of focused, cognitively demanding work with zero distractions — no phone, no notifications, no context switching — for 2 hours.
description_what_to_do_cn: 完成一段专注的、认知要求高的工作，零干扰 — 不看手机、无通知、不切换任务 — 持续 2 小时。
why_it_works_en: Produces the highest-quality cognitive output, builds career-defining skills faster than fragmented work, creates flow states that are intrinsically satisfying, and compounds into a major career advantage over those who cannot focus.
why_it_works_cn: 产生最高质量的认知输出，比碎片化工作更快地建立职业定义技能，创造内在满足的心流状态，并相对于那些无法专注的人累积成重大职业优势。
notes_for_product: Optional text for what you worked on.

two_min_version_en: Log the last thing you spent money on
two_min_version_cn: 记录你最后花钱买的东西
description_what_to_do_en: Record every purchase you make today — item, amount, and category (food, transport, entertainment, etc). Can be done in-app or manually.
description_what_to_do_cn: 记录你今天做的每一笔购买 — 项目、金额和类别（食物、交通、娱乐等）。可以在应用内或手动完成。
why_it_works_en: Creates precise financial awareness. Most people underestimate their discretionary spending by 30–50%. Daily logging reveals patterns (daily coffees, subscriptions, impulse purchases) that are invisible until written down.
why_it_works_cn: 创造精确的财务意识。大多数人低估 30–50% 的可自由支配支出。每日记录揭示直到写下来之前都不可见的模式（每日咖啡、订阅、冲动购买）。
notes_for_product: MUST write item and amount. Category optional.

two_min_version_en: Transfer $1 to savings right now — seriously, just $1
two_min_version_cn: 现在转账 1 美元到储蓄 — 认真的，就 1 美元
description_what_to_do_en: Make a deliberate transfer to a savings account, investment account, or savings goal today. Amount can be any size — the habit is the act, not the amount.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天有意识地转账到储蓄账户、投资账户或储蓄目标。金额可以是任意大小 — 习惯是行为本身，不是金额。
why_it_works_en: Makes saving an active, visible behaviour rather than passive (leftover after spending). 'Pay yourself first' principle dramatically increases savings rates.
why_it_works_cn: 使储蓄成为主动的、可见的行为，而不是被动的（花完剩下的）。'先付给自己'原则显著提高储蓄率。
notes_for_product: Track amount. Progress toward savings goal.

two_min_version_en: Open your bank app and look at this week's spending right now
two_min_version_cn: 打开你的银行应用看看这周的支出
description_what_to_do_en: Once per week, review your spending against your budget: how much did you spend in each category vs what you planned? Adjust next week's plan if needed. Takes 10–15 minutes.
description_what_to_do_cn: 每周一次，对照预算回顾你的支出：每个类别花了多少钱 vs 计划了多少？必要时调整下周计划。需要 10–15 分钟。
why_it_works_en: Creates the feedback loop between spending tracking and intentional planning, surfaces category overspending before it becomes debt, and builds financial competence over time.
why_it_works_cn: 在支出跟踪和有意计划之间建立反馈循环，在超支变成债务之前发现类别超支，随时间建立财务能力。
notes_for_product: MUST write reflection. Weekly frequency.

two_min_version_en: Open your investment app and look at your account balance
two_min_version_cn: 打开你的投资应用看看账户余额
description_what_to_do_en: Make a contribution to an investment account today — index fund, retirement account, crypto, or any investment vehicle. Amount is less important than the consistency of the behaviour.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天向投资账户存入资金 — 指数基金、退休账户、加密货币或任何投资工具。金额不如行为的一致性重要。
why_it_works_en: Compound growth over decades is the primary wealth-building mechanism. Time in market is the most powerful variable. Consistent, automatic, boring investing consistently outperforms active management.
why_it_works_cn: 几十年的复利增长是主要的财富积累机制。在市场中的时间是最强大的变量。一致、自动、枯燥的投资始终优于主动管理。
notes_for_product: Optional text for amount/where.

two_min_version_en: Check your credit score right now — most banks show it for free
two_min_version_cn: 现在查看你的信用评分 — 大多数银行免费提供
description_what_to_do_en: Take one action today to improve your credit score: check your score, make a payment on time, reduce a balance, or set up a new automatic payment. Track your score monthly.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天采取一项改善信用评分的行动：查看你的分数、按时还款、减少余额或设置新的自动付款。每月跟踪你的分数。
why_it_works_en: Credit score affects loan rates, housing applications, and sometimes employment. Even a 50-point improvement can save thousands in mortgage interest. Most people have never actively worked on their score.
why_it_works_cn: 信用评分影响贷款利率、住房申请，有时还影响就业。即使 50 分的提高也能在抵押贷款利息上节省数千美元。大多数人从未主动改善过他们的分数。
notes_for_product: Optional text for score.

two_min_version_en: Pull up your duvet right now — that counts
two_min_version_cn: 现在拉平你的被子 — 这就算
description_what_to_do_en: Make your bed within 30 minutes of waking up. Pull up the sheets, arrange the pillows, and leave the bedroom tidy before starting the day.
description_what_to_do_cn: 起床后 30 分钟内整理床铺。拉平床单，整理枕头，在开始一天之前保持卧室整洁。
why_it_works_en: Gives an immediate sense of accomplishment first thing in the morning, sets an organised tone for the day, improves sense of control and calm, and creates a better sleep environment to return to.
why_it_works_cn: 在早晨第一时间带来即时的成就感，为一天设定有序的基调，提高控制感和平静感，并创造一个更好的睡眠环境。
notes_for_product: Classic keystone habit.

two_min_version_en: Set a 10-minute timer and just start — anywhere
two_min_version_cn: 设置一个 10 分钟的计时器，随便从哪里开始
description_what_to_do_en: Set a timer for 10 minutes and tidy one area of your home or workspace. Do not organise or deep-clean — just put things back where they belong.
description_what_to_do_cn: 设置一个 10 分钟的计时器，整理家中或工作空间的一个区域。不要整理或深度清洁 — 只是把东西放回它们该在的地方。
why_it_works_en: Reduces visual clutter (shown to increase cortisol), improves focus and cognitive performance in the subsequent work session, prevents the accumulation of disorder that leads to overwhelming 'big clean' tasks.
why_it_works_cn: 减少视觉杂乱（已被证明会增加皮质醇），在随后的工作时段提高专注力和认知表现，防止混乱积累导致压倒性的'大扫除'任务。
notes_for_product: Timer-based framing is important.

two_min_version_en: Pick up the first thing you see that you don't need — let it go
two_min_version_cn: 拿起你看到的第一件不需要的东西 — 让它走
description_what_to_do_en: Find one item in your home today that you do not use, need, or love — and either throw it away, donate it, or sell it. Just one thing per day.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天在家里找一件你不用、不需要或不喜欢的物品 — 然后扔掉、捐赠或卖掉它。每天只处理一件。
why_it_works_en: Prevents accumulation of clutter, reduces decision fatigue (fewer possessions = fewer decisions), improves sense of calm and spaciousness, and creates a decluttering identity over time.
why_it_works_cn: 防止杂物堆积，减少决策疲劳（更少的物品 = 更少的决策），提高平静和宽敞感，随时间建立断舍离的身份认同。
notes_for_product: Optional text for what you decluttered.

two_min_version_en: Water one plant right now
two_min_version_cn: 现在给一株植物浇水
description_what_to_do_en: Spend time gardening or tending to plants today — watering, pruning, repotting, planting, or simply observing. Even 5 minutes with a houseplant counts.
description_what_to_do_cn: 今天花时间园艺或照料植物 — 浇水、修剪、换盆、种植或只是观察。即使和室内植物待 5 分钟也算。
why_it_works_en: Gardening reduces cortisol, improves mood, provides light physical activity, connects you to nature, and gives a sense of nurturing and growth. Plant care is associated with reduced depression and anxiety.
why_it_works_cn: 园艺减少皮质醇，改善情绪，提供轻度身体活动，连接自然，并给予养育和成长感。照料植物与减少抑郁和焦虑相关。
notes_for_product: Optional text. For users with plants.
